knownIssues.title: 2448x2448 per eye requires a powerful GPU for PC streaming
knownIssues.description: The Focus Vision's very high resolution places heavy encoding and rendering demands on the PC GPU. Mid-range GPUs may struggle to maintain 90Hz without dropping quality.
knownIssues.workaround: Use RTX 3080 or better for 90Hz streaming at full resolution. Drop VIVE Hub streaming quality to Medium if frame drops occur on lower-end GPUs.

knownIssues.title: Wi-Fi 6 strongly recommended for wireless streaming at this resolution
knownIssues.description: Streaming 2448x2448 per eye wirelessly at acceptable quality requires significantly more bandwidth than lower-resolution headsets. Wi-Fi 5 may produce visible artifacts.
knownIssues.workaround: Use a Wi-Fi 6 router with the headset as the only connected device on that SSID. Wi-Fi 6E on 6GHz is ideal for maximum bandwidth and minimal interference.

knownIssues.title: Some features require HTC enterprise licensing
knownIssues.description: Certain Focus Vision features such as advanced MDM control and enterprise eye tracking APIs require HTC VIVE Enterprise licensing beyond the base hardware cost.
knownIssues.workaround: Consumer PC streaming features do not require enterprise licensing. Contact HTC VIVE Enterprise for feature breakdown.

optimizationTips.tip: The Focus Vision's high resolution shines in productivity and sim titles
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Text, gauges, and fine detail are exceptionally crisp on this headset — great for flight sims and VR productivity.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: At 2448x2448 per eye, the Focus Vision approaches retinal resolution for typical IPDs. SteamVR SS at 100% is sufficient — further supersampling provides no visible benefit and wastes GPU resources. Best used at native resolution in DCS, MSFS, or VR desktop applications.

optimizationTips.tip: Use eye-tracked foveated rendering in supported apps
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Turn on eye tracking foveated rendering in the VIVE Hub to help your GPU focus detail only where you're looking.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The Focus Vision's built-in eye tracking enables foveated rendering via the VIVE Eye Tracking SDK. In supported applications, the central gaze region renders at full resolution while the periphery renders at 50-70%, potentially saving 25-35% GPU render time at this high base resolution.

optimizationTips.tip: USB streaming is preferred for latency-sensitive applications
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Plug in a cable instead of using Wi-Fi when you need the lowest possible delay.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: USB 3.0 streaming via VIVE Hub removes all Wi-Fi variability from the latency chain. At 2448x2448, Wi-Fi has more headroom for worst-case latency spikes under load. For precision work in VR (surgery simulation, design tools), USB streaming is the preferred mode.
